fuzzy_prd:
Hey team, we need a client adapter thingy for that remote AI service we've been integrating with. Basically devs are complaining they keep writing the same boilerplate over and over just to talk to the service — checking if it's up, grabbing providers, running completions, messing with datasets, submitting jobs, getting model recommendations, etc. It's a mess and everyone's doing it slightly differently which causes bugs.

The idea is to build something that wraps all that up cleanly. There should be a way to run it from the command line where you pipe in some JSON describing what you want and it spits out the result — similar to how we did that stdin/stdout pattern on the billing connector last quarter, you know the one. The output format needs to be consistent key=value lines, one per line.

A few things that keep biting us: when a provider isn't in the supported list we need a clear normalized error (not a stack trace), same for blank prompts. Datasets and jobs have their own create/delete/list flows. Oh and the health check output should always include the deployment field even if the service doesn't send it back. For job types we support evaluate and inference routes. Model suggestions can fail with a bad task name and that needs to be handled gracefully too.

Structure should be clean, not one giant file. Tests should run with a single bash command. Let me know if questions.

Quick follow-up from the questions that came in: on the health check, if the service response doesn’t include `deployment_type`, we still need to print `deployment_type=` as an empty value every single time. That field should never disappear from output. That’s the behavior in `available_without_deployment_type`, and it matches the earlier note that the health check output always includes the deployment field even when the service leaves it out.

On vendor validation, if the requested vendor isn’t in the supported vendors list, the output needs to be exactly `error=unsupported_vendor\n`. Nothing else gets printed with it — no HTTP method, no path, no status code lines. This is just our normalized domain error flow for `invalid_vendor`. Also, prompts that are empty or only whitespace need to fail before any HTTP call happens, with exactly `error=empty_prompt\n`, even if the vendor itself is valid.

For job creation, if somebody sends unknown or disallowed fields, that should fail locally too, before any HTTP call is made. The output in that case starts with `error=schema_validation\n` and also includes the failing field name and the validation category. On the model suggestions side, for a valid task like `summarization`, the response should report `total=9` and `item_count=9`, and the first result should be `first_name=facebook/bart-large-cnn` plus `first_uri=hf://facebook/bart-large-cnn`. If the task name is bad, that one comes back as `error=http_status\nstatus_code=400\n`.

A couple implementation-shape details too since folks asked: this should not be one giant file. We want a multi-file layout, like `src/` for the core domain logic and a separate adapter / entry-point script. The stdin/stdout execution adapter is the same pattern described in start.md: read a single JSON object from stdin, route to the core logic, and write key=value lines to stdout. That adapter needs to be physically separate from the business logic. And the HTTP API shape we’re targeting from start.md uses base path `/api/v1/` with these endpoints: `/health`, `/completions`, `/completions/{provider}/`, `/datasets`, `/datasets/{id}`, `/jobs/{type}/`, `/jobs`, `/health/jobs/{submission_id}`, and `/models/{task}`.

Last thing, the IDs in these scenarios are fixed fixture values, not generated on the fly. Dataset and job records use `daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97` as the returned or deleted id, and submission / dataset fields use `6f6487ac-7170-4a11-af7a-0f6db1ec9a74`.

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Text completion POSTs must go to `/api/v1/completions/{provider}/` — note the trailing slash. Example: `path=/api/v1/completions/openai/`. See feature2_2_text_completion.json success cases.

Job type `EVALUATION` maps to path segment `evaluate` (i.e., `/api/v1/jobs/evaluate/`), and `INFERENCE` maps to `inference` (i.e., `/api/v1/jobs/inference/`). Both include a trailing slash. The job_type input is uppercase but the URL segment is lowercase. See feature4_1_job_create.json.

Dataset creation must report `status_code=201` (not 200). All other successful operations (GET, DELETE, job create, etc.) report `status_code=200`. See feature3_2_dataset_write.json scenario `create`.

HTTP failures are rendered as two lines: `error=http_status\n` followed by `status_code={code}\n`. Optionally a response body line may follow if provided. No http_method or path lines are emitted. See feature3_2_dataset_write.json and feature4_2_job_read.json.

Worker submission status must GET `/api/v1/health/jobs/{submission_id}`, not `/api/v1/jobs/{id}`. The output includes `type=SUBMISSION`, `status=PENDING`, and `submission_id=...`. See feature4_2_job_read.json scenario `submission_status` with submission_id `6f6487ac-7170-4a11-af7a-0f6db1ec9a74`.

The full test suite is executed via `bash rcb_tests/test.sh`. It reads all `*.json` files from `rcb_tests/public_test_cases/` by default, or from a custom directory via `--cases-dir <subdir>`. Output files are written to `rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/` named as `{filename.stem}@{case_index.zfill(3)}.txt`.

trigger_keywords: what lines print for zero datasets
hidden_constraints.oracle_response:
An empty dataset listing emits only `http_method=GET
path=/api/v1/datasets
status_code=200
total=0
item_count=0
` — no first_id, first_filename, or first_format lines are included. The same pattern applies to empty job listings. See feature3_1_dataset_read.json scenario `list_empty`.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: Language-neutral error output format: `error={category}\n` optionally followed by `status_code={n}\n`. Categories include: http_status, unsupported_vendor, empty_prompt, schema_validation. No Python exception names or tracebacks.

context_pointers.vague_reference: tests should run with a single bash command
context_pointers.actual_meaning: `bash rcb_tests/test.sh` — the harness reads *.json case files from rcb_tests/public_test_cases/, pipes each case's `input` field as JSON to the adapter, and diffs stdout against `expected_output`. Outputs saved to rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific details on that aspect right now. Please refer to the existing integration patterns in the codebase, particularly how similar features handle their HTTP routing and output formatting, and check the test case JSON files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for the exact expected behavior.

feature1_service_status.json.description: Checks service status responses and normalized failures from the status endpoint.

feature2_1_completion_vendors.json.description: Retrieves the list of available completion providers, including the empty-list case.

feature2_2_text_completion.json.description: Requests generated text from a supported provider and reports normalized validation errors for unsupported providers or blank prompts.

feature3_1_dataset_read.json.description: Reads dataset listings and individual dataset metadata through HTTP endpoints.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=GET
path=/api/v1/datasets
status_code=200
total=3
item_count=3
first_id=daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97
first_filename=ds1.hf
first_format=job

feature3_2_dataset_write.json.description: Creates and deletes dataset records, including normalized not-found delete failures.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=POST
path=/api/v1/datasets
status_code=201
filename=test.hf
format=job
id=daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97

feature4_1_job_create.json.description: Creates evaluation or inference jobs from accepted request fields and reports schema validation for unknown fields.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=POST
path=/api/v1/jobs/evaluate/
status_code=200
id=daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97
name=test-job-001
status=created

http_method=POST
path=/api/v1/jobs/inference/
status_code=200
id=daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97
name=test-job-001
status=created

feature4_2_job_read.json.description: Reads job collections and worker submission status, including normalized missing-submission failures.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=GET
path=/api/v1/jobs
status_code=200
total=3
item_count=3
first_id=daab39ac-be9f-4de9-87c0-c4c94b297a97
first_status=created

feature5_model_suggestions.json.description: Retrieves suggested model metadata for a task and normalizes invalid-task HTTP failures.

cases.expected_output:
http_method=GET
path=/api/v1/models/summarization
status_code=200
total=9
item_count=9
first_name=facebook/bart-large-cnn
first_uri=hf://facebook/bart-large-cnn
